title: Classification axes (type, priority, workstream) + PR-type mapping
description: Formal shape of classification.yaml — three classification axes the methodology mandates on every issue (type, priority, workstream), each with a substrate-with-board / substrate-without-board declaration, allowed values (or `values_project_specific: true` when the value set is open), default-at-filing behaviour, and severities for missing / wrong / duplicated. Plus the top-level `pr_type_mapping` that aligns the closing issue's `type:*` label with the PR title's Conventional Commits `<type>`. Consumed by create-issue (defaults + validation) and validate-body (cross-check classification at edit time). Companion per COR-018.

axes.description: Classification axes. Keys are axis ids (e.g., `type`, `priority`, `workstream`); values match $defs.entry.

pr_type_mapping.description: Bridges the issue-side `type:*` label to the PR-side Conventional Commits `<type>` prefix. One entry per recognised value; multi-issue PRs with mixed types pick the dominant value and warn.

description.description: One-line description of what this axis classifies.

substrate_with_board.description: Where the axis lives when a Projects v2 board is configured — typically a single-select field on the board, or `label` when the axis must live as a label regardless.

substrate_without_board.description: Fallback substrate when no Projects v2 board exists — usually a `<axis>:*` label set.

values.description: Allowed values for this axis. Required when `values_project_specific: false`; omitted when `values_project_specific: true`.

values_project_specific.description: Whether the value set is open and adopter-defined. True for Workstream (project-specific); false for Type and Priority (methodology-fixed sets).

mutually_exclusive.description: Whether at most one value of this axis may apply to a single issue. True for Type (`type:*` is single-valued); typically true for the others.

default_value.description: Value the agent assigns at filing when the user doesn't specify (e.g., `priority:Medium`). Omitted for axes with no sensible default (e.g., Type — inferred from intent — and Workstream — inferred from file paths).

default_inference_strategy.description: When `default_value` is omitted, prose describing how the agent infers a value (e.g., `Type: inferred from filing intent — 'file a bug' → type:bug`; `Workstream: inferred from file paths or topic; agent asks if ambiguous`).

required_on_every_issue.description: Whether every issue must carry this classification. True for all three axes in the methodology; sub-tasks and Milestones are exempt (they're not issues / are a separate primitive).

missing_severity.description: Severity applied when an issue is filed/edited without this axis.

wrong_value_severity.description: Severity applied when an issue carries a value not in `values` (only meaningful when `values_project_specific: false`).

title_prefix_by_value.description: For the `type` axis: maps each kind value to the Task title prefix it carries at the issue level (per project-management:DEC-011-title-formats), e.g. `feature: Task`, `bug: Bug`. Read by create-issue / edit-issue / move-issue to keep the kind label and Task title prefix aligned.

description.description: Prose explaining which structural types each kind may take and why.

allowed_structural_types_per_kind.description: Maps each kind value to the structural types it may take (per project-management:DEC-011-title-formats + DEC-004's hierarchy).

mismatch_severity.description: Severity when a kind is paired with a disallowed structural type.

structural_restriction.description: For the `type` axis: restricts which structural types each kind value may take. Read by edit-issue / move-issue when validating Task-shape issues.

issue_label_value.description: Value of the issue's `type:*` label, e.g., `feature`, `bug`.

pr_conv_type.description: Conventional Commits `<type>` prefix the PR title carries (e.g., `feat`, `fix`).

alternates.description: Other Conventional Commits prefixes acceptable for this issue label (e.g., `chore` may also be `ci` for CI-specific maintenance work).
